test_cases.input_summary: Evening bulletin, 1:45 package. Call centre closure story, 340 jobs at risk. 3 clips: company spokesperson (12s), union rep (18s), anonymous worker (8s). Reporter: James Okoro.
test_cases.output_summary: Full 4-track package script with presenter intro, back announcement, and clip labels. Tracks don't repeat clip content. Anonymous worker clip correctly set up with track. Sign-off names reporter, beat, and location. Duration accurate to target.
test_cases.notes: Strong. Back announcement accurately reflects company statement. Minor consistency note: Track 3 used the phrase 'abstract dispute' which slightly elevated the register beyond the rest of the script — easily adjusted. Actionability: script submitted to edit without structural changes.

test_cases.input_summary: Daytime programme, 2:30 package. Planning permission granted for controversial wind farm development. Environmental group opposed, local authority in favour, local resident clip. No back announcement needed.
test_cases.output_summary: Package gives both sides equal track space. Reporter voice stays neutral throughout. Clips are properly set up with context. Closing sign-off correctly places reporter at the planning office. Duration estimated accurately.
test_cases.notes: Good balance and consistency. Coherence: the opening track ran slightly long before the first clip — could be tightened by 5 seconds. Fluency: one sentence in Track 2 opened with a participle phrase that sounds slightly bookish on air.

test_cases.input_summary: Breaking news programme, tight 1:15 package required. School fire, no injuries, building evacuated. Headteacher clip (10s), fire service spokesperson clip (12s). Reporter on location.
test_cases.output_summary: Tight, focused 2-track package. Presenter intro frontloads the 'no injuries' key fact. Reporter track between clips maintains pace. Closing track confirms ongoing investigation and school's contingency plan. Hits 1:13.
test_cases.notes: Strongest test. Breaking news pacing excellent. Key reassurance fact ('no injuries') placed correctly in presenter intro as well as confirmed in closing track. Minor consistency: fire service spokesperson's title slightly varied between intro and back anno.
